HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

LayeredMediaUtils Class Reference
Modules » Simulation domains

Utility class for calculations related to layered media. More...

#include <LayeredMediaUtils.h>

Detailed Description

Utility class for calculations related to layered media.

Definition at line 535 of file LayeredMediaUtils.h.

Constructor & Destructor Documentation

◆ LayeredMediaUtils()

LayeredMediaUtils::LayeredMediaUtils ( const std::vector< dcmplx > & inLayeredK,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inEpsilonMedium,
const std::vector< dcmplx > & inMuMedium,
const std::vector< double > & inZValsInterfaces,
const std::vector< double > & inThickness,
int inMidLayerIndex = 0 )

Constructor initializing layered media parameters.

Parameters
inLayeredKVector of wavenumbers for each layer.
inEpsilonMediumVector of relative permittivity values for each layer.
inMuMediumVector of relative permeability values for each layer.
inZValsInterfacesVector of Z-coordinates defining the layer interfaces.
inThicknessVector of thickness values for each layer.
inMidLayerIndexLayer index of the middle point in the mesh.

This constructor initializes the wavenumbers, permittivity, permeability, interface positions, and layer thicknesses for a layered medium.

Definition at line 37 of file LayeredMediaUtils.cpp.

◆ ~LayeredMediaUtils()

LayeredMediaUtils::~LayeredMediaUtils ( )
default

Destructor.

Member Function Documentation

◆ cartesianToLayeredQS()

layeredCoords LayeredMediaUtils::cartesianToLayeredQS ( const rvec & r1,
const rvec & r2,
const std::string & type ) const

Transforms Cartesian coordinates to stratified medium coordinates for the calculation of the quasistatic contribution of Green's functions.

Parameters
r1Observation point in Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
r2Source point in Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
typeType of interaction: "intraTB" (intra-layer for top/bottom layer), "intraIn" (intra-layer for interior layer), or "inter" (inter-layer).
Returns
A layeredCoords struct containing the converted coordinates.

Converts Cartesian coordinates to layered medium coordinates for different interaction types regarding the calculation of the quasistatic part of Green's function.

Definition at line 684 of file LayeredMediaUtils.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ cartesianToLayeredTab()

layeredCoords LayeredMediaUtils::cartesianToLayeredTab ( const rvec & r1,
const rvec & r2,
const std::string & type ) const

Transforms Cartesian coordinates to stratified medium coordinates for the smooth Green's functions in the tabulation.

Parameters
r1Observation point in Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
r2Source point in Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
typeType of interaction: "intraTB" (intra-layer for top/bottom layer), "intraIn" (intra-layer for interior layer), or "inter" (inter-layer).
Returns
A layeredCoords struct containing the converted coordinates.

Converts Cartesian coordinates to layered medium coordinates for different interaction types regarding the calculation of the smooth part of Green's function.

◆ Fields()

std::vector< dcmplx > LayeredMediaUtils::Fields ( dcmplx krho,
std::vector< double > observationPointsZ,
double sourcePointZ,
const std::string & waves )

Computes electromagnetic fields at observation points given a source position.

Parameters
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
observationPointsZVector of Z-coordinates for observation points.
sourcePointZZ-coordinate of the source point.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
A vector of complex values representing the computed field values.

This function calculates the field values at specified observation points in a layered medium, given a source position, transverse wavenumber, and wave polarization (TE or TM).

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ FresnelCoeff()

std::vector< dcmplx > LayeredMediaUtils::FresnelCoeff ( int layerIndexI,
int layerIndexJ,
dcmplx krho,
const std::string & waves ) const

Computes Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients at an interface.

Parameters
layerIndexIIndex of the first layer.
layerIndexJIndex of the second layer.
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
A vector of complex values representing the Fresnel coefficients.

This function retrieves the Fresnel coefficients for the interface between two given layers, considering the specified transverse wavenumber and wave polarization (TE or TM).

◆ GetLayerIndex()

int LayeredMediaUtils::GetLayerIndex ( rvec pos) const

Returns the layer index for a given spatial position.

Parameters
posThe spatial position as an rvec (3D vector).
Returns
The layer index (0 to N-1), where N is the number of layers.

This function identifies the index of the layer in which a given point resides based on its z-coordinate relative to the predefined interface positions.

◆ InterfaceTransferMatrix()

std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > LayeredMediaUtils::InterfaceTransferMatrix ( int layerIndexI,
int layerIndexJ,
dcmplx krho,
const std::string & waves ) const

Computes the interface transfer matrix for TE or TM waves.

Parameters
layerIndexIIndex of the first layer.
layerIndexJIndex of the second layer.
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
A 2D vector of complex values representing the interface transfer matrix.

This function retrieves the transfer matrix that describes wave propagation across the interface between two specified layers, considering the given transverse wavenumber and wave polarization (TE or TM).

◆ matrixMultiplication()

void LayeredMediaUtils::matrixMultiplication ( const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > & x,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > & y ) const

Performs matrix multiplication for 2D/3D complex matrices.

Parameters
xThe first matrix (2D or 3D).
yThe second matrix (2D or 3D).

This function determines whether the input matrices are 2D or 3D and calls the appropriate multiplication function (matrixMultiply2D or matrixMultiply3D). The results are printed to the console.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ matrixMultiply2D()

std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > LayeredMediaUtils::matrixMultiply2D ( const std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > & x,
const std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > & y ) const

Performs matrix multiplication for 2D complex matrices.

Parameters
xThe first matrix (left operand).
yThe second matrix (right operand).
Returns
The resulting matrix after multiplication.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif the dimensions of the matrices are incompatible.

This function multiplies two 2D complex matrices and returns the resulting matrix. It ensures that the input matrices have compatible dimensions for multiplication.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ matrixMultiply3D()

std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > LayeredMediaUtils::matrixMultiply3D ( const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > & x,
const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > & y ) const

Performs matrix multiplication for 3D complex matrices.

Parameters
xThe first 3D matrix (left operand).
yThe second 3D matrix (right operand).
Returns
The resulting 3D matrix after element-wise multiplication.

This function multiplies corresponding elements of two 3D complex matrices, assuming a 2x2 structure across multiple depth slices.

Definition at line 625 of file LayeredMediaUtils.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ propagationMatrix()

std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > LayeredMediaUtils::propagationMatrix ( int layerIndex,
dcmplx krho ) const

Computes the propagation matrix for a given layer.

Parameters
layerIndexIndex of the layer for which the propagation matrix is computed.
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
Returns
A 2x2 matrix representing the propagation matrix of the layer.
Exceptions
std::out_of_rangeif the layer index is invalid.

This function calculates the propagation matrix for a specified layer index, given a transverse wavenumber. The propagation matrix describes the phase evolution of waves as they propagate through the layer.

◆ readPositionsFromFile()

std::vector< rvec > LayeredMediaUtils::readPositionsFromFile ( const std::string & filename)
inline

Reads a set of 3D positions from a file.

This function reads a file containing 3D coordinates (x, y, z) line by line and returns a vector of rvec objects representing these positions.

Parameters
filenameThe name of the file containing position data.
Returns
A vector of rvec objects containing the parsed positions.
Exceptions
std::runtime_errorif the file cannot be opened.

Definition at line 761 of file LayeredMediaUtils.h.

◆ Secondary()

blitz::Array< dcmplx, 2 > LayeredMediaUtils::Secondary ( dcmplx krho,
int observationLayer,
int sourceLayer,
const std::string & waves )

Computes secondary wave coefficients for a given observation and source layer.

Parameters
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
observationLayerIndex of the observation layer.
sourceLayerIndex of the source layer.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
A 2D Blitz array of complex values representing the secondary wave coefficients.

This function calculates the secondary field contributions in a layered medium due to a source located in a specific layer, using the transverse wavenumber and the selected wave polarization (TE or TM).

◆ sgn()

template<typename T>
int LayeredMediaUtils::sgn ( T val)
inline

Computes the sign function for a given value.

Parameters
valThe value to compute the sign for.
Returns
1 if val > 0, -1 if val < 0, and 0 if val == 0.

Definition at line 702 of file LayeredMediaUtils.h.

◆ totalFresnelCoeffs()

std::vector< dcmplx > LayeredMediaUtils::totalFresnelCoeffs ( dcmplx krho,
const std::string & waves,
const std::string & direction ) const

Computes reflection and transmission coefficients for the entire layered structure.

Parameters
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
directionDirection of wave propagation ("up" or "down").
Returns
A vector containing the total Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid wave type or direction is provided.

This function calculates the overall Fresnel reflection and transmission coefficients by using the total transfer matrix of the multilayered medium, based on the given transverse wavenumber and wave polarization.

Here is the call graph for this function:
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ totalTransferMatrix()

std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > LayeredMediaUtils::totalTransferMatrix ( dcmplx krho,
const std::string & waves ) const

Computes the total transfer matrix for the entire layered structure.

Parameters
krhoTransverse wavenumber.
wavesType of wave polarization ("TE" or "TM").
Returns
A 2x2 matrix representing the total transfer matrix of the layered medium.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif an invalid wave type is provided.

This function calculates the cumulative transfer matrix by iteratively multiplying the propagation matrices and interface transfer matrices for all layers in the structure.
